            md"!!! warning \"Divergence\"
	Some parameters will not behave as the traditional problem and will lead to some numbers diverging up to infinity. In that case, the calculations will stop at a stopping time of 1000. However, this still can still result in high latency so beware! .

        end
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#447")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64}, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    hailstone_seq = if "Hailstone Sequence" ∈ generalize_collatz
            hailstone_sequence(hailstone_params.start_value; collatz_parameters..., verbose = false)
        else
            hailstone_sequence(hailstone_params.start_value; verbose = false)
        end
    fig_hail = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_hail = Axis(fig_hail[1, 1]; title = "Hailstone sequence of: " * string(hailstone_params.start_value), xlabel = "Iterations", ylabel = "Value")
    hail_x = Float64[]
    hail_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(hailstone_seq)
        #= none:13 =#
        push!(hail_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:14 =#
        push!(hail_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:15 =#
    end
    lines!(ax_hail, hail_x, hail_y; color = (0.678, 0.847, 0.902, 1.0))
    scatter!(ax_hail, hail_x, hail_y; markersize = 12.0, color = (0.678, 0.847, 0.902, 1.0))
    var"##cellval#450" = fig_hail
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#450")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64}, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    md"!!! info \"Numerical Packages\"
	[Collatz](https://juliapackages.com/p/collatz): This package provide the methods to generate the hailstone sequence, the tree graph and stopping time for the collatz conjecture. 

	[Graphs](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/graphs): Used to deal with creating and modifying graphs. 

	[FixedPointNumbers](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/fixedpointnumbers): Package to deal with fixed point number, only used to handle colors.
"
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int=0)\n\nTo handle the case where the search hits a cycle and previous is of type Collatz._CC.CC\n\n## Args \n\n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Collatz._CC.CC`: The cycle value.\n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n\n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:15 =#
            #= none:18 =#
            previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                            #= none:18 =#
                            x == previous
                        end), map((x->begin
                                #= none:18 =#
                                x[2]
                            end), record))
            #= none:21 =#
            if isnothing(previous_index)
                ""
            else
                add_edge!(g, previous, length(record) + 1)
            end
            #= none:24 =#
            push!(record, (depth, key))
            #= none:25 =#
            return
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  tree::Dict, previous::Number=collect(keys(tree))[1], depth::Int=0)\n\t\nThis function is used to explore the tree return by `tree_graph` from Collatz.jl and modify the graph g given as input. \n\n## Args \n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values. Each value is stored as (depth, value) in order to keep track of what depth the value was encountered\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Number`: The number passed by the previous call to the function \n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, tree::Dict, previous::Number = (collect(keys(tree)))[1], depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:13 =#
            #= none:16 =#
            for key = keys(tree)
                #= none:18 =#
                add_vertex!(g)
                #= none:21 =#
                previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                                #= none:21 =#
                                x == previous
                            end), map((x->begin
                                    #= none:21 =#
                                    x[2]
                                end), record))
                #= none:24 =#
                if isnothing(previous_index)
                    ""
                else
                    add_edge!(g, previous_index, length(record) + 1)
                end
                #= none:27 =#
                if key isa Number
                    #= none:28 =#
                    push!(record, (depth, key))
                end
                #= none:32 =#
                descend_tree!(g, record, tree[key], key, depth + 1)
                #= none:33 =#
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tmake_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P=2, a=3, b=1)\n\nThis function returns a graph that represent the different branches that each number takes.\n\n## Args\n\n- `initial_value::Integer`: The starting value of the directed tree graph.\n\n- `max_orbit_distance::Integer`: Degree of seperation between the initial value and each value encountered. \n\n## Kwargs\n\n- ```P::Integer=2```: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n\n- ```a::Integer=3```: Factor by which to multiply n.\n\n- ```b::Integer=1```: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n\n## See also\n[`tree_graph`](@ref)\n" function make_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P = 2, a = 3, b = 1)
            #= none:24 =#
            #= none:25 =#
            g = SimpleGraph()
            #= none:26 =#
            record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}} = []
            #= none:27 =#
            tree = tree_graph(initial_value, max_orbit_distance; P, a, b)
            #= none:28 =#
            descend_tree!(g, record, tree)
            #= none:29 =#
            return (g, record)
        end
    (g, record) = if "Graph" ∈ generalize_collatz
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; collatz_parameters...)
        else
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; )
        end
    graph_colors = [RGB(rescale((record[i])[1], 1, graph_parameters.orbit, 1, 0.3), 0.1, 0.3) for i = 1:nv(g)]
    var"##cellval#451" = graph_colors
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#451")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64}, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    generalize_collatz
    stopping_times = Dict()
    newValues = filter((x->begin
                    #= none:3 =#
                    !(x ∈ keys(stopping_times))
                end), collect(range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)))
    for newValue = newValues
        #= none:7 =#
        push!(stopping_times, newValue => if "Stopping Time" ∈ generalize_collatz
                    stopping_time(newValue; collatz_parameters..., total_stopping_time = true)
                else
                    stopping_time(newValue, total_stopping_time = true)
                end)
        #= none:10 =#
    end
    st_vals = collect(values(sort(filter((key->begin
                                #= none:14 =#
                                key[1] ∈ range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)
                            end), stopping_times))))
    st_x = Float64[]
    st_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(st_vals)
        #= none:21 =#
        push!(st_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:22 =#
        push!(st_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:23 =#
    end
    fig_st = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_st = Axis(fig_st[1, 1]; title = "Total stopping time of numbers up to " * string(stopping_parameters.ub), xlabel = "Starting point", ylabel = "Stopping time")
    scatter!(ax_st, st_x, st_y; markersize = 4.0)
    var"##cellval#453" = fig_st
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#453")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64}, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# @unpack (window_height, window_width) = window_size_parameters
    ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:15 =#
                while n % 2 == 0
                    #= none:16 =#
                    n = n ÷ 2
                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:24 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:25 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:26 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\treverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int=2, a::Int=3, b::Int=1)\nThis function wraps the `hailstone_sequence()` method from Collatz.jl to calculate list of hailstone sequence given a UnitRange. \n\nIt return the reversed sequence where the endpoint is the first element of the result.\n\n## Args\n\n- `range::UnitRange{Int64}`: Unit Range in which to calculate the hailstone sequences.\n\n\n## Kwargs\n\n- `P::Integer = 2`: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n- `a::Integer = 3`: Factor by which to multiply n.\n- `b::Integer = 1`: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n## Examples\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(2:5) \n[[1, 2], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5, 10, 3], [1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5]]\n```\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(1:5; P=4, a=1, b=3)\n[[1], [2, 8, 5, 2], [3, 12, 9, 6, 3], [1, 4], [5, 2, 8, 5]]\n```\n\n## See also\n[`hailstone_sequence`](@ref), [`hailstone_sequences`](@ref)\n" function reverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int = 2, a::Int = 3, b::Int = 1)
            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters
    #= none:4 =# @unpack (stroke_color, background_color) = viz_colors_options
    #= none:5 =# @unpack (random_shade, vary_shade, edmund_style, chris_style) = viz_extra_options
    viz_trajectories = if chris_style
            #= none:9 =#
            [reverse(ultra_shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        elseif #= none:10 =# edmund_style
            #= none:11 =#
            [reverse(shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        else
            #= none:13 =#
            reverse_hailstone_sequences(1:num_traject; P = collatz_parameters.P, a = collatz_parameters.a, b = collatz_parameters.b)
        end
    interactive_viz = let
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (Float64(window_width), Float64(window_height)))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            hidespines!(ax)
            #= none:26 =#
            base_r = Float64(red(stroke_color))
            #= none:27 =#
            base_g = Float64(green(stroke_color))
            #= none:28 =#
            base_b = Float64(blue(stroke_color))
            #= none:30 =#
            for (t_i, seq) = enumerate(viz_trajectories)
                #= none:31 =#
                xs = Float64[]
                #= none:32 =#
                ys = Float64[]
                #= none:33 =#
                x = Float64(x_start)
                #= none:34 =#
                y = Float64(y_start)
                #= none:35 =#
                θ = Float64(init_angle) + 180.0
                #= none:36 =#
                push!(xs, x)
                #= none:37 =#
                push!(ys, y)
                #= none:38 =#
                for v = seq
                    #= none:39 =#
                    if chris_style
                        #= none:40 =#
                        θ += if mod(v, 3) == 1
                                turn_scale
                            else
                                -turn_scale
                            end
                    elseif #= none:41 =# mod(v, 2) == 0
                        #= none:42 =#
                        θ += turn_scale
                    else
                        #= none:44 =#
                        θ -= if edmund_style
                                turn_scale / 2
                            else
                                turn_scale
                            end
                    end
                    #= none:46 =#
                    x += line_length * cosd(θ)
                    #= none:47 =#
                    y += line_length * sind(θ)
                    #= none:48 =#
                    push!(xs, x)
                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64}, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    md"!!! info \"Notebook Packages\"
	[PlutoUI](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/PlutoUI): Extension for Pluto to handle interactivity, provides the Sliders, Checkboxes and Color Picker. 

	[HypertextLiteral](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/HypertextLiteral): Drawing library, specifically for graphs.

"
    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# @unpack (window_height, window_width) = window_size_parameters
    ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:15 =#
                while n % 2 == 0
                    #= none:16 =#
                    n = n ÷ 2
                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:24 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:25 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:26 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\treverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int=2, a::Int=3, b::Int=1)\nThis function wraps the `hailstone_sequence()` method from Collatz.jl to calculate list of hailstone sequence given a UnitRange. \n\nIt return the reversed sequence where the endpoint is the first element of the result.\n\n## Args\n\n- `range::UnitRange{Int64}`: Unit Range in which to calculate the hailstone sequences.\n\n\n## Kwargs\n\n- `P::Integer = 2`: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n- `a::Integer = 3`: Factor by which to multiply n.\n- `b::Integer = 1`: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n## Examples\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(2:5) \n[[1, 2], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5, 10, 3], [1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5]]\n```\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(1:5; P=4, a=1, b=3)\n[[1], [2, 8, 5, 2], [3, 12, 9, 6, 3], [1, 4], [5, 2, 8, 5]]\n```\n\n## See also\n[`hailstone_sequence`](@ref), [`hailstone_sequences`](@ref)\n" function reverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int = 2, a::Int = 3, b::Int = 1)
            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters
    #= none:4 =# @unpack (stroke_color, background_color) = viz_colors_options
    #= none:5 =# @unpack (random_shade, vary_shade, edmund_style, chris_style) = viz_extra_options
    viz_trajectories = if chris_style
            #= none:9 =#
            [reverse(ultra_shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        elseif #= none:10 =# edmund_style
            #= none:11 =#
            [reverse(shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        else
            #= none:13 =#
            reverse_hailstone_sequences(1:num_traject; P = collatz_parameters.P, a = collatz_parameters.a, b = collatz_parameters.b)
        end
    interactive_viz = let
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (Float64(window_width), Float64(window_height)))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            hidespines!(ax)
            #= none:26 =#
            base_r = Float64(red(stroke_color))
            #= none:27 =#
            base_g = Float64(green(stroke_color))
            #= none:28 =#
            base_b = Float64(blue(stroke_color))
            #= none:30 =#
            for (t_i, seq) = enumerate(viz_trajectories)
                #= none:31 =#
                xs = Float64[]
                #= none:32 =#
                ys = Float64[]
                #= none:33 =#
                x = Float64(x_start)
                #= none:34 =#
                y = Float64(y_start)
                #= none:35 =#
                θ = Float64(init_angle) + 180.0
                #= none:36 =#
                push!(xs, x)
                #= none:37 =#
                push!(ys, y)
                #= none:38 =#
                for v = seq
                    #= none:39 =#
                    if chris_style
                        #= none:40 =#
                        θ += if mod(v, 3) == 1
                                turn_scale
                            else
                                -turn_scale
                            end
                    elseif #= none:41 =# mod(v, 2) == 0
                        #= none:42 =#
                        θ += turn_scale
                    else
                        #= none:44 =#
                        θ -= if edmund_style
                                turn_scale / 2
                            else
                                turn_scale
                            end
                    end
                    #= none:46 =#
                    x += line_length * cosd(θ)
                    #= none:47 =#
                    y += line_length * sind(θ)
                    #= none:48 =#
                    push!(xs, x)
                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#
                        (Float64(mod(t_i * 37, 97)) / 97.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 53, 89)) / 89.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 71, 83)) / 83.0, 0.6)
                    elseif #= none:58 =# vary_shade
                        #= none:59 =#
                        shade = (Float64(mod(t_i, 7)) - 3.0) * 0.08
                        #= none:60 =#
                        (clamp(base_r + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_g + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_b + shade, 0.0, 1.0), 0.6)
                    else
                        #= none:64 =#
                        (base_r, base_g, base_b, 0.6)
                    end
                #= none:66 =#
                lines!(ax, xs, ys; color = col, linewidth = Float64(stroke_width))
                #= none:67 =#
            end
            #= none:68 =#
            fig
        end
    var"##cellval#456" = #= none:1 =# @htl("$(PlutoUI.DownloadButton("<!doctype html><html><body>" * html_snippet(interactive_viz) * "</body></html>", if filename == ""
            "MyCoolVisualization"
        else
            filename
        end * ".html"))\n")
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#456")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    var"##cellval#457" = if do_generalize_collatz
            md"!!! warning \"Divergence\"
	Some parameters will not behave as the traditional problem and will lead to some numbers diverging up to infinity. In that case, the calculations will stop at a stopping time of 1000. However, this still can still result in high latency so beware! .
"
        else
            md""
        end
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#457")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    md"!!! info \"Numerical Packages\"
	[Collatz](https://juliapackages.com/p/collatz): This package provide the methods to generate the hailstone sequence, the tree graph and stopping time for the collatz conjecture. 

	[Graphs](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/graphs): Used to deal with creating and modifying graphs. 

	[FixedPointNumbers](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/fixedpointnumbers): Package to deal with fixed point number, only used to handle colors.
"
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int=0)\n\nTo handle the case where the search hits a cycle and previous is of type Collatz._CC.CC\n\n## Args \n\n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Collatz._CC.CC`: The cycle value.\n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n\n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:15 =#
            #= none:18 =#
            previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                            #= none:18 =#
                            x == previous
                        end), map((x->begin
                                #= none:18 =#
                                x[2]
                            end), record))
            #= none:21 =#
            if isnothing(previous_index)
                ""
            else
                add_edge!(g, previous, length(record) + 1)
            end
            #= none:24 =#
            push!(record, (depth, key))
            #= none:25 =#
            return
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  tree::Dict, previous::Number=collect(keys(tree))[1], depth::Int=0)\n\t\nThis function is used to explore the tree return by `tree_graph` from Collatz.jl and modify the graph g given as input. \n\n## Args \n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values. Each value is stored as (depth, value) in order to keep track of what depth the value was encountered\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Number`: The number passed by the previous call to the function \n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, tree::Dict, previous::Number = (collect(keys(tree)))[1], depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:13 =#
            #= none:16 =#
            for key = keys(tree)
                #= none:18 =#
                add_vertex!(g)
                #= none:21 =#
                previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                                #= none:21 =#
                                x == previous
                            end), map((x->begin
                                    #= none:21 =#
                                    x[2]
                                end), record))
                #= none:24 =#
                if isnothing(previous_index)
                    ""
                else
                    add_edge!(g, previous_index, length(record) + 1)
                end
                #= none:27 =#
                if key isa Number
                    #= none:28 =#
                    push!(record, (depth, key))
                end
                #= none:32 =#
                descend_tree!(g, record, tree[key], key, depth + 1)
                #= none:33 =#
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tmake_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P=2, a=3, b=1)\n\nThis function returns a graph that represent the different branches that each number takes.\n\n## Args\n\n- `initial_value::Integer`: The starting value of the directed tree graph.\n\n- `max_orbit_distance::Integer`: Degree of seperation between the initial value and each value encountered. \n\n## Kwargs\n\n- ```P::Integer=2```: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n\n- ```a::Integer=3```: Factor by which to multiply n.\n\n- ```b::Integer=1```: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n\n## See also\n[`tree_graph`](@ref)\n" function make_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P = 2, a = 3, b = 1)
            #= none:24 =#
            #= none:25 =#
            g = SimpleGraph()
            #= none:26 =#
            record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}} = []
            #= none:27 =#
            tree = tree_graph(initial_value, max_orbit_distance; P, a, b)
            #= none:28 =#
            descend_tree!(g, record, tree)
            #= none:29 =#
            return (g, record)
        end
    (g, record) = if "Graph" ∈ generalize_collatz
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; collatz_parameters...)
        else
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; )
        end
    graph_colors = [RGB(rescale((record[i])[1], 1, graph_parameters.orbit, 1, 0.3), 0.1, 0.3) for i = 1:nv(g)]
    var"##cellval#461" = graph_colors
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#461")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, filename::String, viz_colors_options::Vector{String}, viz_extra_options::@NamedTuple{random_shade::Bool, vary_shade::Bool, edmund_style::Bool, chris_style::Bool}, viz_parameters::@NamedTuple{num_traject::Float64, line_length::Int64, turn_scale::Float64}, viz_specs_parameters::@NamedTuple{init_angle::Float64, x_start::Float64, y_start::Float64, stroke_width::Float64}, window_size_parameters::@NamedTuple{window_height::Float64, window_width::Float64})
    generalize_collatz
    stopping_times = Dict()
    newValues = filter((x->begin
                    #= none:3 =#
                    !(x ∈ keys(stopping_times))
                end), collect(range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)))
    for newValue = newValues
        #= none:7 =#
        push!(stopping_times, newValue => if "Stopping Time" ∈ generalize_collatz
                    stopping_time(newValue; collatz_parameters..., total_stopping_time = true)
                else
                    stopping_time(newValue, total_stopping_time = true)
                end)
        #= none:10 =#
    end
    st_vals = collect(values(sort(filter((key->begin
                                #= none:14 =#
                                key[1] ∈ range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)
                            end), stopping_times))))
    st_x = Float64[]
    st_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(st_vals)
        #= none:21 =#
        push!(st_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:22 =#
        push!(st_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:23 =#
    end
    fig_st = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_st = Axis(fig_st[1, 1]; title = "Total stopping time of numbers up to " * string(stopping_parameters.ub), xlabel = "Starting point", ylabel = "Stopping time")
    scatter!(ax_st, st_x, st_y; markersize = 4.0)
    var"##cellval#463" = fig_st
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#463")
end

    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# @unpack (window_height, window_width) = window_size_parameters
    ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:15 =#
                while n % 2 == 0
                    #= none:16 =#
                    n = n ÷ 2
                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence

                #= none:24 =#

            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters
    #= none:4 =# @unpack (stroke_color, background_color) = viz_colors_options
    #= none:5 =# @unpack (random_shade, vary_shade, edmund_style, chris_style) = viz_extra_options
    viz_trajectories = if chris_style
            #= none:9 =#
            [reverse(ultra_shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        elseif #= none:10 =# edmund_style
            #= none:11 =#
            [reverse(shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        else
            #= none:13 =#
            reverse_hailstone_sequences(1:num_traject; P = collatz_parameters.P, a = collatz_parameters.a, b = collatz_parameters.b)
        end
    interactive_viz = let
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (Float64(window_width), Float64(window_height)))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            hidespines!(ax)
            #= none:26 =#
            base_r = Float64(red(stroke_color))
            #= none:27 =#
            base_g = Float64(green(stroke_color))
            #= none:28 =#
            base_b = Float64(blue(stroke_color))
            #= none:30 =#
            for (t_i, seq) = enumerate(viz_trajectories)
                #= none:31 =#
                xs = Float64[]
                #= none:32 =#
                ys = Float64[]
                #= none:33 =#
                x = Float64(x_start)
                #= none:34 =#
                y = Float64(y_start)
                #= none:35 =#
                θ = Float64(init_angle) + 180.0
                #= none:36 =#
                push!(xs, x)
                #= none:37 =#
                push!(ys, y)
                #= none:38 =#
                for v = seq
                    #= none:39 =#
                    if chris_style
                        #= none:40 =#
                        θ += if mod(v, 3) == 1
                                turn_scale
                            else
                                -turn_scale
                            end
                    elseif #= none:41 =# mod(v, 2) == 0
                        #= none:42 =#
                        θ += turn_scale
                    else
                        #= none:44 =#
                        θ -= if edmund_style
                                turn_scale / 2
                            else
                                turn_scale
                            end
                    end
                    #= none:46 =#
                    x += line_length * cosd(θ)
                    #= none:47 =#
                    y += line_length * sind(θ)
                    #= none:48 =#
                    push!(xs, x)
                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#
                        (Float64(mod(t_i * 37, 97)) / 97.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 53, 89)) / 89.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 71, 83)) / 83.0, 0.6)
                    elseif #= none:58 =# vary_shade
                        #= none:59 =#
                        shade = (Float64(mod(t_i, 7)) - 3.0) * 0.08
                        #= none:60 =#
                        (clamp(base_r + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_g + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_b + shade, 0.0, 1.0), 0.6)
                    else
                        #= none:64 =#
                        (base_r, base_g, base_b, 0.6)
                    end
                #= none:66 =#
                lines!(ax, xs, ys; color = col, linewidth = Float64(stroke_width))
                #= none:67 =#
            end
            #= none:68 =#
            fig
        end
    var"##cellval#465" = interactive_viz
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#465")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64})
    hailstone_seq = if "Hailstone Sequence" ∈ generalize_collatz
            hailstone_sequence(hailstone_params.start_value; collatz_parameters..., verbose = false)
        else
            hailstone_sequence(hailstone_params.start_value; verbose = false)
        end
    fig_hail = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_hail = Axis(fig_hail[1, 1]; title = "Hailstone sequence of: " * string(hailstone_params.start_value), xlabel = "Iterations", ylabel = "Value")
    hail_x = Float64[]
    hail_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(hailstone_seq)
        #= none:13 =#
        push!(hail_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:14 =#
        push!(hail_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:15 =#
    end
    lines!(ax_hail, hail_x, hail_y; color = (0.678, 0.847, 0.902, 1.0))
    scatter!(ax_hail, hail_x, hail_y; markersize = 12.0, color = (0.678, 0.847, 0.902, 1.0))
    var"##cellval#469" = fig_hail
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#469")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64})
    md"!!! info \"Numerical Packages\"
	[Collatz](https://juliapackages.com/p/collatz): This package provide the methods to generate the hailstone sequence, the tree graph and stopping time for the collatz conjecture. 

	[Graphs](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/graphs): Used to deal with creating and modifying graphs. 

	[FixedPointNumbers](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/fixedpointnumbers): Package to deal with fixed point number, only used to handle colors.
"
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int=0)\n\nTo handle the case where the search hits a cycle and previous is of type Collatz._CC.CC\n\n## Args \n\n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Collatz._CC.CC`: The cycle value.\n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n\n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:15 =#
            #= none:18 =#
            previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                            #= none:18 =#
                            x == previous
                        end), map((x->begin
                                #= none:18 =#
                                x[2]
                            end), record))
            #= none:21 =#
            if isnothing(previous_index)
                ""
            else
                add_edge!(g, previous, length(record) + 1)
            end
            #= none:24 =#
            push!(record, (depth, key))
            #= none:25 =#
            return
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  tree::Dict, previous::Number=collect(keys(tree))[1], depth::Int=0)\n\t\nThis function is used to explore the tree return by `tree_graph` from Collatz.jl and modify the graph g given as input. \n\n## Args \n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values. Each value is stored as (depth, value) in order to keep track of what depth the value was encountered\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Number`: The number passed by the previous call to the function \n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, tree::Dict, previous::Number = (collect(keys(tree)))[1], depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:13 =#
            #= none:16 =#
            for key = keys(tree)
                #= none:18 =#
                add_vertex!(g)
                #= none:21 =#
                previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                                #= none:21 =#
                                x == previous
                            end), map((x->begin
                                    #= none:21 =#
                                    x[2]
                                end), record))
                #= none:24 =#
                if isnothing(previous_index)
                    ""
                else
                    add_edge!(g, previous_index, length(record) + 1)
                end
                #= none:27 =#
                if key isa Number
                    #= none:28 =#
                    push!(record, (depth, key))
                end
                #= none:32 =#
                descend_tree!(g, record, tree[key], key, depth + 1)
                #= none:33 =#
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tmake_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P=2, a=3, b=1)\n\nThis function returns a graph that represent the different branches that each number takes.\n\n## Args\n\n- `initial_value::Integer`: The starting value of the directed tree graph.\n\n- `max_orbit_distance::Integer`: Degree of seperation between the initial value and each value encountered. \n\n## Kwargs\n\n- ```P::Integer=2```: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n\n- ```a::Integer=3```: Factor by which to multiply n.\n\n- ```b::Integer=1```: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n\n## See also\n[`tree_graph`](@ref)\n" function make_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P = 2, a = 3, b = 1)
            #= none:24 =#
            #= none:25 =#
            g = SimpleGraph()
            #= none:26 =#
            record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}} = []
            #= none:27 =#
            tree = tree_graph(initial_value, max_orbit_distance; P, a, b)
            #= none:28 =#
            descend_tree!(g, record, tree)
            #= none:29 =#
            return (g, record)
        end
    (g, record) = if "Graph" ∈ generalize_collatz
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; collatz_parameters...)
        else
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; )
        end
    graph_colors = [RGB(rescale((record[i])[1], 1, graph_parameters.orbit, 1, 0.3), 0.1, 0.3) for i = 1:nv(g)]
    var"##cellval#470" = graph_colors
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#470")

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64})
    generalize_collatz
    stopping_times = Dict()
    newValues = filter((x->begin
                    #= none:3 =#
                    !(x ∈ keys(stopping_times))
                end), collect(range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)))
    for newValue = newValues
        #= none:7 =#
        push!(stopping_times, newValue => if "Stopping Time" ∈ generalize_collatz
                    stopping_time(newValue; collatz_parameters..., total_stopping_time = true)
                else
                    stopping_time(newValue, total_stopping_time = true)
                end)
        #= none:10 =#
    end
    st_vals = collect(values(sort(filter((key->begin
                                #= none:14 =#
                                key[1] ∈ range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)
                            end), stopping_times))))
    st_x = Float64[]
    st_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(st_vals)
        #= none:21 =#
        push!(st_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:22 =#
        push!(st_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:23 =#
    end
    fig_st = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_st = Axis(fig_st[1, 1]; title = "Total stopping time of numbers up to " * string(stopping_parameters.ub), xlabel = "Starting point", ylabel = "Stopping time")
    scatter!(ax_st, st_x, st_y; markersize = 4.0)
    var"##cellval#472" = fig_st
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#472")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64})
    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    window_height = $(QuoteNode(700.0))
    window_width = $(QuoteNode(500.0))
    ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:15 =#
                while n % 2 == 0
                    #= none:16 =#
                    n = n ÷ 2
                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:24 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:25 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:26 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\treverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int=2, a::Int=3, b::Int=1)\nThis function wraps the `hailstone_sequence()` method from Collatz.jl to calculate list of hailstone sequence given a UnitRange. \n\nIt return the reversed sequence where the endpoint is the first element of the result.\n\n## Args\n\n- `range::UnitRange{Int64}`: Unit Range in which to calculate the hailstone sequences.\n\n\n## Kwargs\n\n- `P::Integer = 2`: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n- `a::Integer = 3`: Factor by which to multiply n.\n- `b::Integer = 1`: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n## Examples\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(2:5) \n[[1, 2], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5, 10, 3], [1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5]]\n```\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(1:5; P=4, a=1, b=3)\n[[1], [2, 8, 5, 2], [3, 12, 9, 6, 3], [1, 4], [5, 2, 8, 5]]\n```\n\n## See also\n[`hailstone_sequence`](@ref), [`hailstone_sequences`](@ref)\n" function reverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int = 2, a::Int = 3, b::Int = 1)
            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters
    #= none:4 =# @unpack (stroke_color, background_color) = viz_colors_options
    #= none:5 =# @unpack (random_shade, vary_shade, edmund_style, chris_style) = viz_extra_options
    viz_trajectories = if chris_style
            #= none:9 =#
            [reverse(ultra_shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        elseif #= none:10 =# edmund_style
            #= none:11 =#
            [reverse(shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        else
            #= none:13 =#
            reverse_hailstone_sequences(1:num_traject; P = collatz_parameters.P, a = collatz_parameters.a, b = collatz_parameters.b)
        end
    interactive_viz = let
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (Float64(window_width), Float64(window_height)))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            hidespines!(ax)
            #= none:26 =#
            base_r = Float64(red(stroke_color))
            #= none:27 =#
            base_g = Float64(green(stroke_color))
            #= none:28 =#
            base_b = Float64(blue(stroke_color))
            #= none:30 =#
            for (t_i, seq) = enumerate(viz_trajectories)
                #= none:31 =#
                xs = Float64[]
                #= none:32 =#
                ys = Float64[]
                #= none:33 =#
                x = Float64(x_start)
                #= none:34 =#
                y = Float64(y_start)
                #= none:35 =#
                θ = Float64(init_angle) + 180.0
                #= none:36 =#
                push!(xs, x)
                #= none:37 =#
                push!(ys, y)
                #= none:38 =#
                for v = seq
                    #= none:39 =#
                    if chris_style
                        #= none:40 =#
                        θ += if mod(v, 3) == 1
                                turn_scale
                            else
                                -turn_scale
                            end
                    elseif #= none:41 =# mod(v, 2) == 0
                        #= none:42 =#
                        θ += turn_scale
                    else
                        #= none:44 =#
                        θ -= if edmund_style
                                turn_scale / 2
                            else
                                turn_scale
                            end
                    end
                    #= none:46 =#
                    x += line_length * cosd(θ)
                    #= none:47 =#
                    y += line_length * sind(θ)
                    #= none:48 =#
                    push!(xs, x)
                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#
                        (Float64(mod(t_i * 37, 97)) / 97.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 53, 89)) / 89.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 71, 83)) / 83.0, 0.6)
                    elseif #= none:58 =# vary_shade
                        #= none:59 =#
                        shade = (Float64(mod(t_i, 7)) - 3.0) * 0.08
                        #= none:60 =#
                        (clamp(base_r + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_g + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_b + shade, 0.0, 1.0), 0.6)
                    else
                        #= none:64 =#
                        (base_r, base_g, base_b, 0.6)
                    end
                #= none:66 =#
                lines!(ax, xs, ys; color = col, linewidth = Float64(stroke_width))
                #= none:67 =#
            end
            #= none:68 =#
            fig
        end
    var"##cellval#473" = interactive_viz
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#473")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64})
    md"!!! info \"Numerical Packages\"
	[Collatz](https://juliapackages.com/p/collatz): This package provide the methods to generate the hailstone sequence, the tree graph and stopping time for the collatz conjecture. 

	[Graphs](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/graphs): Used to deal with creating and modifying graphs. 

	[FixedPointNumbers](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/fixedpointnumbers): Package to deal with fixed point number, only used to handle colors.
"
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int=0)\n\nTo handle the case where the search hits a cycle and previous is of type Collatz._CC.CC\n\n## Args \n\n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Collatz._CC.CC`: The cycle value.\n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n\n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:15 =#
            #= none:18 =#
            previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                            #= none:18 =#
                            x == previous
                        end), map((x->begin
                                #= none:18 =#
                                x[2]
                            end), record))
            #= none:21 =#
            if isnothing(previous_index)
                ""
            else
                add_edge!(g, previous, length(record) + 1)
            end
            #= none:24 =#
            push!(record, (depth, key))
            #= none:25 =#
            return
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  tree::Dict, previous::Number=collect(keys(tree))[1], depth::Int=0)\n\t\nThis function is used to explore the tree return by `tree_graph` from Collatz.jl and modify the graph g given as input. \n\n## Args \n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values. Each value is stored as (depth, value) in order to keep track of what depth the value was encountered\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Number`: The number passed by the previous call to the function \n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, tree::Dict, previous::Number = (collect(keys(tree)))[1], depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:13 =#
            #= none:16 =#
            for key = keys(tree)
                #= none:18 =#
                add_vertex!(g)
                #= none:21 =#
                previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                                #= none:21 =#
                                x == previous
                            end), map((x->begin
                                    #= none:21 =#
                                    x[2]
                                end), record))
                #= none:24 =#
                if isnothing(previous_index)
                    ""
                else
                    add_edge!(g, previous_index, length(record) + 1)
                end
                #= none:27 =#
                if key isa Number
                    #= none:28 =#
                    push!(record, (depth, key))
                end
                #= none:32 =#
                descend_tree!(g, record, tree[key], key, depth + 1)
                #= none:33 =#
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tmake_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P=2, a=3, b=1)\n\nThis function returns a graph that represent the different branches that each number takes.\n\n## Args\n\n- `initial_value::Integer`: The starting value of the directed tree graph.\n\n- `max_orbit_distance::Integer`: Degree of seperation between the initial value and each value encountered. \n\n## Kwargs\n\n- ```P::Integer=2```: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n\n- ```a::Integer=3```: Factor by which to multiply n.\n\n- ```b::Integer=1```: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n\n## See also\n[`tree_graph`](@ref)\n" function make_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P = 2, a = 3, b = 1)
            #= none:24 =#
            #= none:25 =#
            g = SimpleGraph()
            #= none:26 =#

            descend_tree!(g, record, tree)
            #= none:29 =#
            return (g, record)
        end
    (g, record) = if "Graph" ∈ generalize_collatz
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; collatz_parameters...)
        else

    graph_colors = [RGB(rescale((record[i])[1], 1, graph_parameters.orbit, 1, 0.3), 0.1, 0.3) for i = 1:nv(g)]
    var"##cellval#478" = graph_colors
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#478")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64})
    generalize_collatz
    stopping_times = Dict()
    newValues = filter((x->begin
                    #= none:3 =#
                    !(x ∈ keys(stopping_times))
                end), collect(range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)))
    for newValue = newValues
        #= none:7 =#
        push!(stopping_times, newValue => if "Stopping Time" ∈ generalize_collatz
                    stopping_time(newValue; collatz_parameters..., total_stopping_time = true)
                else
                    stopping_time(newValue, total_stopping_time = true)
                end)
        #= none:10 =#
    end
    st_vals = collect(values(sort(filter((key->begin
                                #= none:14 =#
                                key[1] ∈ range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)
                            end), stopping_times))))
    st_x = Float64[]

    for (i, v) = enumerate(st_vals)
        #= none:21 =#
        push!(st_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:22 =#
        push!(st_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:23 =#
    end
    fig_st = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_st = Axis(fig_st[1, 1]; title = "Total stopping time of numbers up to " * string(stopping_parameters.ub), xlabel = "Starting point", ylabel = "Stopping time")

    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#480")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, graph_parameters::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64, orbit::Int64})
    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    window_height = $(QuoteNode(700.0))
    window_width = $(QuoteNode(500.0))
    ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:15 =#
                while n % 2 == 0
                    #= none:16 =#
                    n = n ÷ 2
                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:24 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:25 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:26 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\treverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int=2, a::Int=3, b::Int=1)\nThis function wraps the `hailstone_sequence()` method from Collatz.jl to calculate list of hailstone sequence given a UnitRange. \n\nIt return the reversed sequence where the endpoint is the first element of the result.\n\n## Args\n\n- `range::UnitRange{Int64}`: Unit Range in which to calculate the hailstone sequences.\n\n\n## Kwargs\n\n- `P::Integer = 2`: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n- `a::Integer = 3`: Factor by which to multiply n.\n- `b::Integer = 1`: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n## Examples\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(2:5) \n[[1, 2], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5, 10, 3], [1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5]]\n```\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(1:5; P=4, a=1, b=3)\n[[1], [2, 8, 5, 2], [3, 12, 9, 6, 3], [1, 4], [5, 2, 8, 5]]\n```\n\n## See also\n[`hailstone_sequence`](@ref), [`hailstone_sequences`](@ref)\n" function reverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int = 2, a::Int = 3, b::Int = 1)
            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters
    #= none:4 =# @unpack (stroke_color, background_color) = viz_colors_options
    #= none:5 =# @unpack (random_shade, vary_shade, edmund_style, chris_style) = viz_extra_options
    viz_trajectories = if chris_style
            #= none:9 =#
            [reverse(ultra_shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        elseif #= none:10 =# edmund_style
            #= none:11 =#
            [reverse(shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        else
            #= none:13 =#
            reverse_hailstone_sequences(1:num_traject; P = collatz_parameters.P, a = collatz_parameters.a, b = collatz_parameters.b)
        end
    interactive_viz = let
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (Float64(window_width), Float64(window_height)))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            hidespines!(ax)
            #= none:26 =#
            base_r = Float64(red(stroke_color))
            #= none:27 =#
            base_g = Float64(green(stroke_color))
            #= none:28 =#
            base_b = Float64(blue(stroke_color))
            #= none:30 =#
            for (t_i, seq) = enumerate(viz_trajectories)
                #= none:31 =#
                xs = Float64[]
                #= none:32 =#
                ys = Float64[]
                #= none:33 =#
                x = Float64(x_start)
                #= none:34 =#
                y = Float64(y_start)
                #= none:35 =#
                θ = Float64(init_angle) + 180.0
                #= none:36 =#
                push!(xs, x)
                #= none:37 =#
                push!(ys, y)
                #= none:38 =#
                for v = seq
                    #= none:39 =#
                    if chris_style
                        #= none:40 =#
                        θ += if mod(v, 3) == 1
                                turn_scale
                            else
                                -turn_scale
                            end
                    elseif #= none:41 =# mod(v, 2) == 0
                        #= none:42 =#
                        θ += turn_scale
                    else
                        #= none:44 =#
                        θ -= if edmund_style
                                turn_scale / 2
                            else
                                turn_scale
                            end
                    end
                    #= none:46 =#
                    x += line_length * cosd(θ)
                    #= none:47 =#
                    y += line_length * sind(θ)
                    #= none:48 =#
                    push!(xs, x)
                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#
                        (Float64(mod(t_i * 37, 97)) / 97.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 53, 89)) / 89.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 71, 83)) / 83.0, 0.6)
                    elseif #= none:58 =# vary_shade
                        #= none:59 =#
                        shade = (Float64(mod(t_i, 7)) - 3.0) * 0.08
                        #= none:60 =#
                        (clamp(base_r + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_g + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_b + shade, 0.0, 1.0), 0.6)
                    else
                        #= none:64 =#
                        (base_r, base_g, base_b, 0.6)
                    end
                #= none:66 =#
                lines!(ax, xs, ys; color = col, linewidth = Float64(stroke_width))
                #= none:67 =#
            end
            #= none:68 =#
            fig
        end
    var"##cellval#481" = interactive_viz
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#481")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64})
