cells.fn_src:
function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64})
    md"!!! info \"Numerical Packages\"
	[Collatz](https://juliapackages.com/p/collatz): This package provide the methods to generate the hailstone sequence, the tree graph and stopping time for the collatz conjecture. 

	[Graphs](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/graphs): Used to deal with creating and modifying graphs. 

	[FixedPointNumbers](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/fixedpointnumbers): Package to deal with fixed point number, only used to handle colors.
"
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int=0)\n\nTo handle the case where the search hits a cycle and previous is of type Collatz._CC.CC\n\n## Args \n\n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Collatz._CC.CC`: The cycle value.\n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n\n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:15 =#
            #= none:18 =#
            previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                            #= none:18 =#
                            x == previous
                        end), map((x->begin
                                #= none:18 =#
                                x[2]
                            end), record))
            #= none:21 =#
            if isnothing(previous_index)
                ""
            else
                add_edge!(g, previous, length(record) + 1)
            end
            #= none:24 =#
            push!(record, (depth, key))
            #= none:25 =#
            return
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  tree::Dict, previous::Number=collect(keys(tree))[1], depth::Int=0)\n\t\nThis function is used to explore the tree return by `tree_graph` from Collatz.jl and modify the graph g given as input. \n\n## Args \n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values. Each value is stored as (depth, value) in order to keep track of what depth the value was encountered\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Number`: The number passed by the previous call to the function \n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, tree::Dict, previous::Number = (collect(keys(tree)))[1], depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:13 =#
            #= none:16 =#
            for key = keys(tree)
                #= none:18 =#
                add_vertex!(g)
                #= none:21 =#
                previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                                #= none:21 =#
                                x == previous
                            end), map((x->begin
                                    #= none:21 =#
                                    x[2]
                                end), record))
                #= none:24 =#
                if isnothing(previous_index)
                    ""
                else
                    add_edge!(g, previous_index, length(record) + 1)
                end
                #= none:27 =#
                if key isa Number
                    #= none:28 =#
                    push!(record, (depth, key))
                end
                #= none:32 =#
                descend_tree!(g, record, tree[key], key, depth + 1)
                #= none:33 =#
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tmake_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P=2, a=3, b=1)\n\nThis function returns a graph that represent the different branches that each number takes.\n\n## Args\n\n- `initial_value::Integer`: The starting value of the directed tree graph.\n\n- `max_orbit_distance::Integer`: Degree of seperation between the initial value and each value encountered. \n\n## Kwargs\n\n- ```P::Integer=2```: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n\n- ```a::Integer=3```: Factor by which to multiply n.\n\n- ```b::Integer=1```: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n\n## See also\n[`tree_graph`](@ref)\n" function make_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P = 2, a = 3, b = 1)
            #= none:24 =#
            #= none:25 =#
            g = SimpleGraph()
            #= none:26 =#
            record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}} = []
            #= none:27 =#
            tree = tree_graph(initial_value, max_orbit_distance; P, a, b)
            #= none:28 =#
            descend_tree!(g, record, tree)
            #= none:29 =#
            return (g, record)
        end
    (g, record) = if "Graph" ∈ generalize_collatz
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; collatz_parameters...)
        else
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; )
        end
    graph_colors = [RGB(rescale((record[i])[1], 1, graph_parameters.orbit, 1, 0.3), 0.1, 0.3) for i = 1:nv(g)]
    var"##cellval#486" = graph_colors
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#486")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64})
    generalize_collatz
    stopping_times = Dict()
    newValues = filter((x->begin
                    #= none:3 =#
                    !(x ∈ keys(stopping_times))
                end), collect(range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)))
    for newValue = newValues
        #= none:7 =#
        push!(stopping_times, newValue => if "Stopping Time" ∈ generalize_collatz
                    stopping_time(newValue; collatz_parameters..., total_stopping_time = true)
                else
                    stopping_time(newValue, total_stopping_time = true)
                end)
        #= none:10 =#
    end
    st_vals = collect(values(sort(filter((key->begin
                                #= none:14 =#
                                key[1] ∈ range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)
                            end), stopping_times))))
    st_x = Float64[]
    st_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(st_vals)
        #= none:21 =#
        push!(st_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:22 =#
        push!(st_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:23 =#
    end
    fig_st = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_st = Axis(fig_st[1, 1]; title = "Total stopping time of numbers up to " * string(stopping_parameters.ub), xlabel = "Starting point", ylabel = "Stopping time")
    scatter!(ax_st, st_x, st_y; markersize = 4.0)
    var"##cellval#488" = fig_st
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#488")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, hailstone_params::@NamedTuple{start_value::Int64})
    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    window_height = $(QuoteNode(700.0))
    window_width = $(QuoteNode(500.0))
    ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:15 =#
                while n % 2 == 0
                    #= none:16 =#
                    n = n ÷ 2
                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:24 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:25 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:26 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\treverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int=2, a::Int=3, b::Int=1)\nThis function wraps the `hailstone_sequence()` method from Collatz.jl to calculate list of hailstone sequence given a UnitRange. \n\nIt return the reversed sequence where the endpoint is the first element of the result.\n\n## Args\n\n- `range::UnitRange{Int64}`: Unit Range in which to calculate the hailstone sequences.\n\n\n## Kwargs\n\n- `P::Integer = 2`: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n- `a::Integer = 3`: Factor by which to multiply n.\n- `b::Integer = 1`: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n## Examples\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(2:5) \n[[1, 2], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5, 10, 3], [1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5]]\n```\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(1:5; P=4, a=1, b=3)\n[[1], [2, 8, 5, 2], [3, 12, 9, 6, 3], [1, 4], [5, 2, 8, 5]]\n```\n\n## See also\n[`hailstone_sequence`](@ref), [`hailstone_sequences`](@ref)\n" function reverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int = 2, a::Int = 3, b::Int = 1)
            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters
    #= none:4 =# @unpack (stroke_color, background_color) = viz_colors_options
    #= none:5 =# @unpack (random_shade, vary_shade, edmund_style, chris_style) = viz_extra_options
    viz_trajectories = if chris_style
            #= none:9 =#
            [reverse(ultra_shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        elseif #= none:10 =# edmund_style
            #= none:11 =#
            [reverse(shortcut_collatz(n)) for n = 1:num_traject]
        else
            #= none:13 =#
            reverse_hailstone_sequences(1:num_traject; P = collatz_parameters.P, a = collatz_parameters.a, b = collatz_parameters.b)
        end
    interactive_viz = let
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (Float64(window_width), Float64(window_height)))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            hidespines!(ax)
            #= none:26 =#
            base_r = Float64(red(stroke_color))
            #= none:27 =#
            base_g = Float64(green(stroke_color))
            #= none:28 =#
            base_b = Float64(blue(stroke_color))
            #= none:30 =#
            for (t_i, seq) = enumerate(viz_trajectories)
                #= none:31 =#
                xs = Float64[]
                #= none:32 =#
                ys = Float64[]
                #= none:33 =#
                x = Float64(x_start)
                #= none:34 =#
                y = Float64(y_start)
                #= none:35 =#
                θ = Float64(init_angle) + 180.0
                #= none:36 =#
                push!(xs, x)
                #= none:37 =#
                push!(ys, y)
                #= none:38 =#
                for v = seq
                    #= none:39 =#
                    if chris_style
                        #= none:40 =#
                        θ += if mod(v, 3) == 1
                                turn_scale
                            else
                                -turn_scale
                            end
                    elseif #= none:41 =# mod(v, 2) == 0
                        #= none:42 =#
                        θ += turn_scale
                    else
                        #= none:44 =#
                        θ -= if edmund_style
                                turn_scale / 2
                            else
                                turn_scale
                            end
                    end
                    #= none:46 =#
                    x += line_length * cosd(θ)
                    #= none:47 =#
                    y += line_length * sind(θ)
                    #= none:48 =#
                    push!(xs, x)
                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#
                        (Float64(mod(t_i * 37, 97)) / 97.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 53, 89)) / 89.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 71, 83)) / 83.0, 0.6)
                    elseif #= none:58 =# vary_shade
                        #= none:59 =#
                        shade = (Float64(mod(t_i, 7)) - 3.0) * 0.08
                        #= none:60 =#
                        (clamp(base_r + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_g + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_b + shade, 0.0, 1.0), 0.6)
                    else
                        #= none:64 =#
                        (base_r, base_g, base_b, 0.6)
                    end
                #= none:66 =#
                lines!(ax, xs, ys; color = col, linewidth = Float64(stroke_width))
                #= none:67 =#
            end
            #= none:68 =#
            fig
        end
    var"##cellval#489" = interactive_viz
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#489")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64})
    md"!!! info \"Numerical Packages\"
	[Collatz](https://juliapackages.com/p/collatz): This package provide the methods to generate the hailstone sequence, the tree graph and stopping time for the collatz conjecture. 

	[Graphs](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/graphs): Used to deal with creating and modifying graphs. 

	[FixedPointNumbers](https://www.juliapackages.com/p/fixedpointnumbers): Package to deal with fixed point number, only used to handle colors.
"
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int=0)\n\nTo handle the case where the search hits a cycle and previous is of type Collatz._CC.CC\n\n## Args \n\n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Collatz._CC.CC`: The cycle value.\n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n\n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, key::Int64, previous::Collatz._CC.CC, depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:15 =#
            #= none:18 =#
            previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                            #= none:18 =#
                            x == previous
                        end), map((x->begin
                                #= none:18 =#
                                x[2]
                            end), record))
            #= none:21 =#
            if isnothing(previous_index)
                ""
            else
                add_edge!(g, previous, length(record) + 1)
            end
            #= none:24 =#
            push!(record, (depth, key))
            #= none:25 =#
            return
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tdescend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}},  tree::Dict, previous::Number=collect(keys(tree))[1], depth::Int=0)\n\t\nThis function is used to explore the tree return by `tree_graph` from Collatz.jl and modify the graph g given as input. \n\n## Args \n- `g::SimpleGraph`: The graph to modify \n- `record::Array{Tuple{Number,Number}}`: An array that keeps track of each of the encountered values. Each value is stored as (depth, value) in order to keep track of what depth the value was encountered\n- `tree::Dict`: The tree graph returned by `tree_graph` \n- `previous::Number`: The number passed by the previous call to the function \n- `depth::Int`: The current depth of the search  \n" function descend_tree!(g::SimpleGraph{Int64}, record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}}, tree::Dict, previous::Number = (collect(keys(tree)))[1], depth::Int = 0)
            #= none:13 =#
            #= none:16 =#
            for key = keys(tree)
                #= none:18 =#
                add_vertex!(g)
                #= none:21 =#
                previous_index = findfirst((x->begin
                                #= none:21 =#
                                x == previous
                            end), map((x->begin
                                    #= none:21 =#
                                    x[2]
                                end), record))
                #= none:24 =#
                if isnothing(previous_index)
                    ""
                else
                    add_edge!(g, previous_index, length(record) + 1)
                end
                #= none:27 =#
                if key isa Number
                    #= none:28 =#
                    push!(record, (depth, key))
                end
                #= none:32 =#
                descend_tree!(g, record, tree[key], key, depth + 1)
                #= none:33 =#
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tmake_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P=2, a=3, b=1)\n\nThis function returns a graph that represent the different branches that each number takes.\n\n## Args\n\n- `initial_value::Integer`: The starting value of the directed tree graph.\n\n- `max_orbit_distance::Integer`: Degree of seperation between the initial value and each value encountered. \n\n## Kwargs\n\n- ```P::Integer=2```: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n\n- ```a::Integer=3```: Factor by which to multiply n.\n\n- ```b::Integer=1```: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n\n## See also\n[`tree_graph`](@ref)\n" function make_collatz_graph(initial_value::Int, max_orbit_distance::Int; P = 2, a = 3, b = 1)
            #= none:24 =#
            #= none:25 =#
            g = SimpleGraph()
            #= none:26 =#
            record::Array{Tuple{Number, Number}} = []
            #= none:27 =#
            tree = tree_graph(initial_value, max_orbit_distance; P, a, b)
            #= none:28 =#
            descend_tree!(g, record, tree)
            #= none:29 =#
            return (g, record)
        end
    (g, record) = if "Graph" ∈ generalize_collatz
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; collatz_parameters...)
        else
            make_collatz_graph(graph_parameters.start_value, graph_parameters.orbit; )
        end
    graph_colors = [RGB(rescale((record[i])[1], 1, graph_parameters.orbit, 1, 0.3), 0.1, 0.3) for i = 1:nv(g)]
    var"##cellval#494" = graph_colors
    return (PlutoIslands._plain_body)(var"##cellval#494")
end

function (collatz_parameters::@NamedTuple{P::Int64, a::Int64, b::Int64}, do_generalize_collatz::Bool, generalize_collatz::Vector{String}, stopping_parameters::@NamedTuple{ub::Int64})
    generalize_collatz
    stopping_times = Dict()
    newValues = filter((x->begin
                    #= none:3 =#
                    !(x ∈ keys(stopping_times))
                end), collect(range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)))
    for newValue = newValues
        #= none:7 =#
        push!(stopping_times, newValue => if "Stopping Time" ∈ generalize_collatz
                    stopping_time(newValue; collatz_parameters..., total_stopping_time = true)
                else
                    stopping_time(newValue, total_stopping_time = true)
                end)
        #= none:10 =#
    end
    st_vals = collect(values(sort(filter((key->begin
                                #= none:14 =#
                                key[1] ∈ range(1, stopping_parameters.ub, step = 1)
                            end), stopping_times))))
    st_x = Float64[]
    st_y = Float64[]
    for (i, v) = enumerate(st_vals)
        #= none:21 =#
        push!(st_x, Float64(i))
        #= none:22 =#
        push!(st_y, Float64(v))
        #= none:23 =#
    end
    fig_st = Figure(size = (640, 420))
    ax_st = Axis(fig_st[1, 1]; title = "Total stopping time of numbers up to " * string(stopping_parameters.ub), xlabel = "Starting point", ylabel = "Stopping time")
    scatter!(ax_st, st_x, st_y; markersize = 4.0)
    var"##cellval#496" = fig_st
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#496")
end

    shortcut_collatz_cache = Dict{Int, Vector{Int}}()
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tshortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2 if odd and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(shortcut_collatz_cache, n)
                #= none:12 =#
                return shortcut_collatz_cache[n]
            elseif #= none:13 =# n % 2 == 0
                #= none:14 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(n ÷ 2)...]
                #= none:15 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:16 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:18 =#
                sequence = [n, shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:19 =#
                shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:20 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    window_height = $(QuoteNode(700.0))
    window_width = $(QuoteNode(500.0))

    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\tultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)\n\nCalculate the collatz sequence of a number using the absolute shortcut formulation:\ng(n) = (3n + 1) / 2^k  if odd where k is the highest power that divides 3n+1 and g(n) = n / 2 if even\n\n" function ultra_shortcut_collatz(n::Int)
            #= none:8 =#
            #= none:9 =#
            if n == 1
                #= none:10 =#
                return [1]
            elseif #= none:11 =# haskey(ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache, n)

                    #= none:17 =#
                end
                #= none:19 =#
                if n == 1
                    #= none:19 =#
                    return [1]
                end
                #= none:20 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(3n + 1)...]
                #= none:21 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:22 =#
                return sequence
            else
                #= none:24 =#
                sequence = [n, ultra_shortcut_collatz(Int((3n + 1) / 2))...]
                #= none:25 =#
                ultra_shortcut_collatz_cache[n] = sequence
                #= none:26 =#
                return sequence
            end
        end
    #= none:1 =# Core.@doc "\treverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int=2, a::Int=3, b::Int=1)\nThis function wraps the `hailstone_sequence()` method from Collatz.jl to calculate list of hailstone sequence given a UnitRange. \n\nIt return the reversed sequence where the endpoint is the first element of the result.\n\n## Args\n\n- `range::UnitRange{Int64}`: Unit Range in which to calculate the hailstone sequences.\n\n\n## Kwargs\n\n- `P::Integer = 2`: Modulus used to devide n, iff n is equivalent to (0 mod P).\n- `a::Integer = 3`: Factor by which to multiply n.\n- `b::Integer = 1`: Value to add to the scaled value of n.\n\n## Examples\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(2:5) \n[[1, 2], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5, 10, 3], [1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 5]]\n```\n```jldoctest\njulia> hailstone_sequences(1:5; P=4, a=1, b=3)\n[[1], [2, 8, 5, 2], [3, 12, 9, 6, 3], [1, 4], [5, 2, 8, 5]]\n```\n\n## See also\n[`hailstone_sequence`](@ref), [`hailstone_sequences`](@ref)\n" function reverse_hailstone_sequences(range::UnitRange{Int64}; P::Int = 2, a::Int = 3, b::Int = 1)
            #= none:31 =#
            #= none:32 =#
            return [reverse(hailstone_sequence(starting_number; P, a, b, verbose = false)) for starting_number = range]
        end
    #= none:2 =# @unpack (num_traject, turn_scale, line_length) = viz_parameters
    #= none:3 =# @unpack (init_angle, x_start, y_start, stroke_width) = viz_specs_parameters

                    #= none:46 =#

                    #= none:47 =#

                    #= none:49 =#
                    push!(ys, y)
                    #= none:50 =#
                end
                #= none:54 =#
                col = if random_shade
                        #= none:55 =#
                        (Float64(mod(t_i * 37, 97)) / 97.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 53, 89)) / 89.0, Float64(mod(t_i * 71, 83)) / 83.0, 0.6)
                    elseif #= none:58 =# vary_shade
                        #= none:59 =#
                        shade = (Float64(mod(t_i, 7)) - 3.0) * 0.08
                        #= none:60 =#
                        (clamp(base_r + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_g + shade, 0.0, 1.0), clamp(base_b + shade, 0.0, 1.0), 0.6)
                    else
                        #= none:64 =#
                        (base_r, base_g, base_b, 0.6)
                    end

                #= none:67 =#

function standard_Newton(f, n, x_range, x0, ymin = -10.0, ymax = 10.0)
    #= none:1 =#
    #= none:3 =#
    x0 = Float64(x0)
    #= none:5 =#

                #= none:5 =#

    xs = Float64[]
    #= none:8 =#
    ys = Float64[]
    #= none:11 =#
    let t = Float64(first(x_range)), hi = Float64(last(x_range)), dt = Float64(step(x_range))
        #= none:13 =#
        while t <= hi + dt / 2
            #= none:14 =#
            push!(xs, t)
            #= none:15 =#
            push!(ys, Float64(f(t)))
            #= none:16 =#
            t += dt
            #= none:17 =#
        end
    end
    #= none:20 =#
    fig = Figure(size = (400, 300))
    #= none:21 =#
    ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
    #= none:22 =#
    ax.ymin = Float64(ymin)
    #= none:23 =#
    ax.ymax = Float64(ymax)
    #= none:25 =#
    lines!(ax, xs, ys; linewidth = 3)
    #= none:26 =#
    hlines!(ax, [0.0]; color = :magenta, linewidth = 3, linestyle = :dash)
    #= none:27 =#
    scatter!(ax, [Float64(x0)], [0.0]; color = :green)
    #= none:29 =#
    for i = 1:n
        #= none:31 =#
        lines!(ax, [Float64(x0), Float64(x0)], [0.0, Float64(f(x0))]; color = (0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5))
        #= none:33 =#
        scatter!(ax, [Float64(x0)], [Float64(f(x0))]; color = :red)
        #= none:34 =#
        m = f′(x0)
        #= none:36 =#
        ts = Float64[]
        #= none:37 =#
        for x = xs
            #= none:38 =#
            push!(ts, Float64(straight(x0, f(x0), x, m)))
            #= none:39 =#
        end
        #= none:40 =#
        lines!(ax, xs, ts; color = (0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 0.5), linestyle = :dash, linewidth = 2)
        #= none:43 =#
        x1 = x0 - f(x0) / m
        #= none:45 =#
        scatter!(ax, [Float64(x1)], [0.0]; color = :green)
        #= none:47 =#
        x0 = x1
        #= none:49 =#
    end
    #= none:51 =#
    fig
end

    #= none:3 =#

    #= none:7 =#

function (c_for_fractal::ComplexF64, color1::String, color2::String)
    img_size = $(QuoteNode(300))
    var"##cellval#878" = let
            #= none:4 =#
            coords = Float64[]
            #= none:5 =#
            for k = 0:img_size
                #= none:6 =#
                push!(coords, -1.5 + (3.0k) / img_size)
                #= none:7 =#
            end
            #= none:9 =#
            fractal = julia_fractal(80, coords, coords, c_for_fractal, Complex)
            #= none:11 =#
            c1 = (Float64(red(color1)), Float64(green(color1)), Float64(blue(color1)), 1.0)
            #= none:12 =#
            c2 = (Float64(red(color2)), Float64(green(color2)), Float64(blue(color2)), 1.0)
            #= none:15 =#
            pixels = Vector{NTuple{4, Float64}}(undef, img_size * img_size)
            #= none:16 =#
            for j = 1:img_size, i = 1:img_size
                #= none:17 =#
                v = fractal[j + (i - 1) * (img_size + 1)]
                #= none:18 =#
                pixels[i + (j - 1) * img_size] = if v == 1.0f0
                        c1
                    else
                        c2
                    end
                #= none:19 =#
            end
            #= none:21 =#
            fig = Figure(size = (340, 340))
            #= none:22 =#
            ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
            #= none:23 =#
            hidedecorations!(ax)
            #= none:24 =#
            image!(ax, (-1.5, 1.5), (-1.5, 1.5), pixels, img_size, img_size; interpolate = false)
            #= none:25 =#
            fig
        end
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#878")
end

function (c_for_fractal::ComplexF64, color1::String, color2::String) return ((("

Recipe: Julia Set picture

  1. Choose a complex number \$c\$ (on the left).

  2. We define our favorite sequence \$z_{n+1} = z_n^2 + c\$

  3. Now, we're going over all the imaginary numbers in a square around \$0\$, and for each:

  • Use the number as the initial value to compute a sequence.

  • If the sequence converges, we color the point in " * string(color1)) * ".

  • If the sequence diverges, we color the point in ") * string(color2)) * ".

" end

"

Recipe: Julia Set picture

  1. Choose a complex number \$c\$ (on the left).

  2. We define our favorite sequence \$z_{n+1} = z_n^2 + c\$

  3. Now, we're going over all the imaginary numbers in a square around \$0\$, and for each:

  • Use the number as the initial value to compute a sequence.

  • If the sequence converges, we color the point in #f5c894.

  • If the sequence diverges, we color the point in #445233.

"

preamble:
f(z, c) = begin
        #= none:2 =#
        z * z + c
    end

function is_stable(iterations, z, c)
    #= none:6 =#
    #= none:7 =#
    for _ = 1:iterations
        #= none:9 =#
        if abs(z) > 5
            #= none:10 =#
            return 0
        end
        #= none:12 =#
        z = f(z, c)
        #= none:13 =#
    end
    #= none:14 =#
    1
end

function julia_fractal(depth, X, Y, c, f)
    #= none:21 =#
    #= none:22 =#
    img = fill(0.0f0, (img_size + 1) * (img_size + 1))
    #= none:23 =#
    for x = X
        #= none:24 =#
        for y = Y
            #= none:25 =#
            z = f(x, y)
            #= none:26 =#
            (x_scaled, y_scaled) = scale_tuple((x, y), 0, img_size)
            #= none:27 =#
            img[y_scaled + 1 + x_scaled * (img_size + 1)] = is_stable(depth, z, c)
            #= none:28 =#
        end
        #= none:29 =#
    end
    #= none:30 =#
    img
end

    patients = if exponential
            (collect(["👵", "🧑👴", "🧑🧔🏼🧝🧚", "👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓", "👵🧝🧑👴🧑🧝🧝🧚🧓🧚🧓🧚🧑🧝🧝🧝", "👵🧝🧑👴🧑👵🧝🧑👴🧑🧓🧚👩🧝🧚👩👩🧝🧚👩👴👴👩🧝🧚🧓🧚🧓👴👵🧑🧑", "🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧓🧚🧓🧚🧑🧝🧝👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓👵🧝🧑👴🧑👵🧝🧑👴🧑🧓🧚👩🧝🧚👩👩🧝🧚👩👴👴👩🧝🧚🧓🧚🧓👴👵🧑🧑🧑", ""]))[1:len]
        else
            (collect(["👵", "👴🧑", "👩🧔🏼🧝🧚", "👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓", "👵🧝🧑👴", "🧓🧚", "🧚", ""]))[1:len]
        end
    patients_flipped = append!(["" for i = 1:8 - length(patients)], reverse(patients))
    var"##cellval#935" = patients_flipped
    return var"##cellval#935"
end

function (a1_s::Int64, a2_s::Int64, a3_s::Int64, a4_s::Int64, a5_s::Int64, a6_s::Int64, a7_s::Int64, a8_s::Int64, exponential::Bool, k_conv::Int64, len::Int64, stairs::Bool)
    treatment_in = [a1_s, a2_s, a3_s, a4_s, a5_s, a6_s, a7_s, a8_s]
    treatment = vec([repeat('💊', i) for i = treatment_in])
    var"##cellval#937" = treatment
    return var"##cellval#937"
end

function (a1_s::Int64, a2_s::Int64, a3_s::Int64, a4_s::Int64, a5_s::Int64, a6_s::Int64, a7_s::Int64, a8_s::Int64, exponential::Bool, k_conv::Int64, len::Int64, stairs::Bool)
    patients = if exponential
            (collect(["👵", "🧑👴", "🧑🧔🏼🧝🧚", "👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓", "👵🧝🧑👴🧑🧝🧝🧚🧓🧚🧓🧚🧑🧝🧝🧝", "👵🧝🧑👴🧑👵🧝🧑👴🧑🧓🧚👩🧝🧚👩👩🧝🧚👩👴👴👩🧝🧚🧓🧚🧓👴👵🧑🧑", "🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧚🧓🧚🧓🧚🧑🧝🧝👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓👵🧝🧑👴🧑👵🧝🧑👴🧑🧓🧚👩🧝🧚👩👩🧝🧚👩👴👴👩🧝🧚🧓🧚🧓👴👵🧑🧑🧑", ""]))[1:len]
        else
            (collect(["👵", "👴🧑", "👩🧔🏼🧝🧚", "👧👴👴👩🧝🧚👩🧓", "👵🧝🧑👴", "🧓🧚", "🧚", ""]))[1:len]
        end
    treatment_in = [a1_s, a2_s, a3_s, a4_s, a5_s, a6_s, a7_s, a8_s]
    treatment = vec([repeat('💊', i) for i = treatment_in])
    var"##cellval#938" = let
            #= none:2 =#
            t = treatment
            #= none:3 =#
            p = patients
            #= none:4 =#
            md"Now as a doctor, you want to be ready for this scenario, at each day you want to know, how many pills you will need for your patients:

- On day 1: you would give $((t[1]))  to $((p[1])) 
- On day 2: you would give $((t[2]))  to $((p[1]))  , and $((t[1]))  to $((p[2]))  each
- On day 3: you would give  $((t[3]))  to $(length(p[1])) , and $((t[2])) pills to $((p[2])) , and $((t[1]))  to $((p[3]))  each ... etc 

Notice how we are multiplying and adding each day? We are close to performing a convolution already!
"
        end
    return (PlutoIslands._html_body)(var"##cellval#938")
end

preamble: using PlutoUI.ExperimentalLayout: grid, vbox, hbox, Div

function draw_point(x, count, ax::Axis, style::Symbol, size_marker, color::NTuple{4, Float64}, offset::Int, masked = false)
    #= none:3 =#
    #= none:4 =#
    for y = 1:count[1]
        #= none:5 =#
        if style == :patient
            #= none:6 =#
            scatter!(ax, [Float64(x) + 0.1], [Float64(y + offset)]; markersize = Float64(size_marker), marker = :circle, color = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0))
            #= none:9 =#
            scatter!(ax, [Float64(x) + 0.1], [Float64(y + offset)]; markersize = 0.62 * Float64(size_marker), marker = :circle, color = color)
        elseif #= none:11 =# style == :pillghost
            #= none:12 =#
            scatter!(ax, [Float64(x) - 0.2], [Float64(y + offset)]; markersize = Float64(size_marker), marker = :circle, color = (0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.2))
        else
            #= none:16 =#
            scatter!(ax, [Float64(x) - 0.2], [Float64(y + offset)]; markersize = Float64(size_marker), marker = :circle, color = color)
        end
        #= none:19 =#
    end
end

function simple_conv(a, b)
    #= none:3 =#
    #= none:4 =#
    n = length(a)
    #= none:5 =#
    m = length(b)
    #= none:6 =#
    out = fill(0.0, (n + m) - 1)
    #= none:7 =#
    for i = 1:n
        #= none:8 =#
        for j = 1:m
            #= none:9 =#
            out[(i + j) - 1] += Float64(a[i]) * Float64(b[j])
            #= none:10 =#
        end
        #= none:11 =#
    end
    #= none:12 =#
    out
end

function draw_all(l, k, k_conv, x, x_conv, y1, y2, y2_draw, y12, y3, ax1, ax2, ax3, grey_marker, color_marker, masked = false)
    #= none:1 =#
    #= none:2 =#
    offset = 0
    #= none:3 =#
    for index = k:k + nb
        #= none:4 =#
        scatter!(ax3, [Float64(x_conv[k_conv])], [Float64(y3[k_conv])]; color = colors[1])
        #= none:6 =#
        if index < 1 || index > l
            #= none:7 =#
            continue
        end
        #= none:10 =#
        color = colors[mod(index, col_length) + 1]
        #= none:13 =#
        draw_point(x[index], y12[index], ax2, :pill, color_marker, color, 0)
        #= none:14 =#
        draw_point(x_conv[k_conv], y12[index], ax3, :pill, color_marker, color, offset)
        #= none:16 =#
        draw_point(x[index], y12[index], ax2, :pillghost, grey_marker, color, 0)
        #= none:18 =#
        if y1[index] != 0 && y2_draw[index] != 0
            #= none:19 =#
            draw_point(x[index], y2_draw[index], ax1, :patient, grey_marker, color, 0, masked)
        end
        #= none:22 =#
        offset += y12[index]
        #= none:23 =#
    end
end

function draw_grey_points(x, y, ax, len, style, size_marker, colors, masked = false)
    #= none:1 =#
    #= none:3 =#
    for j = 1:len
        #= none:4 =#
        color = colors[mod(j, length(colors)) + 1]
        #= none:5 =#
        draw_point(x[j], y[j], ax, style, size_marker, color, 0, masked)
        #= none:7 =#
    end
end

function convolve(img, filter)
    #= none:1 =#
    #= none:4 =#
    (img_row, img_col) = size(img)
    #= none:5 =#
    (filter_row, filter_col) = size(filter)
    #= none:8 =#
    filtered = zeros((img_row - filter_row) + 1, (img_col - filter_col) + 1)
    #= none:9 =#
    (filtered_row, filtered_col) = size(filtered)
    #= none:12 =#
    for i = 1:filtered_row
        #= none:13 =#
        for j = 1:filtered_col
            #= none:14 =#
            filtered[i, j] = sum(img[i:(i + filter_row) - 1, j:(j + filter_col) - 1] .* filter)
            #= none:15 =#
        end
        #= none:16 =#
    end
    #= none:18 =#
    return filtered
end

function demonstrate_filter(image, filter)
    #= none:2 =#
    #= none:3 =#
    (height, width) = size(image)
    #= none:4 =#
    filtered_img = Gray.(convolve(image, filter))

function rgb_figure(m::AbstractMatrix; px::Int = 240)
    #= none:2 =#
    #= none:3 =#
    small = m[1:ceil(Int, size(m, 1) / px):end, 1:ceil(Int, size(m, 2) / px):end]
    #= none:4 =#
    (nrows, ncols) = size(small)
    #= none:5 =#
    (nrows == 0 || ncols == 0) && return Figure(size = (80, 80))
    #= none:6 =#
    pixels = Vector{NTuple{4, Float64}}(undef, nrows * ncols)
    #= none:7 =#
    for i = 1:nrows, j = 1:ncols
        #= none:8 =#
        c = RGBA{Float64}(small[i, j])
        #= none:9 =#
        pixels[j + (nrows - i) * ncols] = (Float64(red(c)), Float64(green(c)), Float64(blue(c)), Float64(alpha(c)))
        #= none:11 =#
    end
    #= none:12 =#
    target = if max(nrows, ncols) <= 8
            80.0
        else
            360.0
        end
    #= none:13 =#
    s = target / max(nrows, ncols)
    #= none:14 =#
    fig = Figure(size = (max(round(Int, ncols * s), 24), max(round(Int, nrows * s), 24)))
    #= none:15 =#
    ax = Axis(fig[1, 1])
    #= none:16 =#
    hidedecorations!(ax)
    #= none:17 =#
    hidespines!(ax)
    #= none:18 =#
    image!(ax, (0, ncols), (0, nrows), pixels, Int64(ncols), Int64(nrows); interpolate = false)
    #= none:20 =#
    HTML(WasmMakie.html_snippet(fig; fonts = false))
end

rgb_figure(v::AbstractVector{<:Colorant}; kwargs...) = begin
        #= none:22 =#
        rgb_figure(permutedims(v); kwargs...)
    end

rgb_figure(c::Colorant; kwargs...) = begin
        #= none:23 =#
        rgb_figure(fill(c, 1, 1); kwargs...)
    end

function forward!(t::Turtle, d)
    #= none:17 =#
    #= none:18 =#
    x2 = t.pos[1] + Float64(d) * sind(t.heading)
    #= none:19 =#
    y2 = t.pos[2] + Float64(d) * cosd(t.heading)
    #= none:20 =#

            #= none:25 =#
            push!(t.ys[end], y2)
        else
            #= none:27 =#
            push!(t.xs, [t.pos[1], x2])
            #= none:28 =#
            push!(t.ys, [t.pos[2], y2])
            #= none:29 =#
            push!(t.cols, t.color)
        end
    end
    #= none:32 =#
    t.pos = (x2, y2)
    #= none:33 =#
    t

backward!(t::Turtle, d) = begin
        #= none:35 =#

right!(t::Turtle, a) = begin

function color!(t::Turtle, c::String)
    #= none:41 =#
    #= none:42 =#
    t.color = if c == "black"
            (0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
        else
            if c == "white"
                (1.0, 1.0, 1.0, 1.0)
            else
                if c == "red"
                    (1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
                else
                    if c == "yellow"
                        (1.0, 1.0, 0.0, 1.0)
                    else
                        if c == "blue"
                            (0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0)
                        else
                            (0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    #= none:49 =#
    t
end

function color_hsl!(t::Turtle, h, s, l)
    #= none:53 =#
    #= none:54 =#
    hf = mod(Float64(h), 360.0) / 60.0
    #= none:55 =#
    c = (1.0 - abs(2.0l - 1.0)) * s
    #= none:56 =#
    x = c * (1.0 - abs(mod(hf, 2.0) - 1.0))
    #= none:57 =#
    m = l - c / 2.0
    #= none:58 =#
    (r, g, b) = if hf < 1.0
            (c, x, 0.0)
        else
            if hf < 2.0
                (x, c, 0.0)
            else
                if hf < 3.0
                    (0.0, c, x)
                else
                    if hf < 4.0
                        (0.0, x, c)
                    else
                        if hf < 5.0
                            (x, 0.0, c)
                        else
                            (c, 0.0, x)
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
    #= none:64 =#
    t.color = (r + m, g + m, b + m, 1.0)
    #= none:65 =#
    t
end

function turtle_drawing(f::Function; background::String = "white")

    #= none:69 =#

    ax.xmin = -15.0
    #= none:75 =#
    ax.xmax = 15.0
    #= none:76 =#
    ax.ymin = -15.0
    #= none:76 =#
    ax.ymax = 15.0
    #= none:77 =#
    if background != "white"
        #= none:78 =#
        bg = if background == "#000088"
                (0.0, 0.0, 8.0 / 15.0, 1.0)

            end
        #= none:79 =#
        hspan!(ax, [-15.0], [15.0]; color = bg)

    #= none:81 =#

    end
    #= none:84 =#

function calc_pH(v_Base_added::Float64)
    #= none:3 =#
    #= none:4 =#
    if pKa <= 1.74
        #= none:5 =#
        if v_Base_added * c0_Base <= v0_Acid * c0_Acid
            #= none:6 =#
            c_H⁺::Float64 = (c0_Acid * v0_Acid - c0_Base * v_Base_added) / (v0_Acid + v_Base_added) + 10 ^ -7
        else
            #= none:8 =#
            c_OH⁻::Float64 = (c0_Base * v_Base_added - c0_Acid * v0_Acid) / (v0_Acid + v_Base_added)
            #= none:9 =#
            c_H⁺ = 10 ^ -14 / c_OH⁻
        end
        #= none:11 =#
        pH = -(log(10, c_H⁺))
    else
        #= none:13 =#
        if v_Base_added == 0.0
            #= none:14 =#
            pH = -(log(10, c0_H⁺))
        elseif #= none:15 =# round((c0_Acid - c0_H⁺) * v0_Acid, digits = 3) > round(c0_Base * v_Base_added, digits = 3)
            #= none:16 =#
            c_H⁺ = Ka * ((c0_Acid * v0_Acid) / (c0_H⁺ * v0_Acid + c0_Base * v_Base_added) - 1)
            #= none:17 =#
            pH = -(log(10, c_H⁺))
        elseif #= none:18 =# round((c0_Acid - c0_H⁺) * v0_Acid, digits = 3) == round(c0_Base * v_Base_added, digits = 3)
            #= none:19 =#
            pH = 14 - -(log(10, c0_OH⁻))
        elseif #= none:20 =# round((c0_Acid - c0_H⁺) * v0_Acid, digits = 3) < round(c0_Base * v_Base_added, digits = 3)
            #= none:21 =#
            c_OH⁻ = (c0_Base * v_Base_added - (c0_Acid - c0_H⁺) * v0_Acid) / (v0_Acid + v_Base_added) + c0_OH⁻
            #= none:22 =#
            c_H⁺ = 10 ^ -14 / c_OH⁻
            #= none:23 =#
            pH = -(log(10, c_H⁺))
        end
    end
end
